rationale: In the conceptual background, the citing paper uses Goldsmith and Amir (2010) to support a core premise that uncertainty can induce optimistic expectations. That premise is then used to build the paper's mechanism about how consolation prizes shift attention to probability and reduce perceived chances of winning the grand prize. The citation is therefore part of theory framing and hypothesis logic, not just a passing literature-list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Goldsmith and Amir (2010) as conceptual groundwork for optimism under uncertainty in promotion evaluations. It builds from that baseline to show that adding high-probability consolation prizes can dampen that optimism and lower lottery valuations.
